title: Cluster Networking, CNI, NetworkPolicy, Dual Stack, and Layered Troubleshooting · Guided Manifest Studio
summary: A platform pairing session predicts each API transition before producing the first working network intent and isolation evidence policy for cluster networking, cni, networkpolicy, dual stack, and layered troubleshooting. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of network intent and isolation evidence policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace Pod addressing, routing, CNI responsibilities, Service implementation, node paths, NAT, MTU, and platform-specific exceptions.
Interpret single or dual-stack families, family policy, ClusterIPs, EndpointSlices, service proxies, source addresses, and asymmetric reachability.
Derive ingress and egress isolation from Pod and namespace selectors, ipBlock, ports, peers, policy types, and additive allow rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Cni Cluster Network Model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Trace Pod addressing, routing, CNI responsibilities, Service implementation, node paths, NAT, MTU, and platform-specific exceptions.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Cni Cluster Network Model.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace pod addressing, routing, cni responsibilities, service implementation, node paths, nat, mtu, and platform-specific exceptions. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Cni Cluster Network Model, keep K8s Cni Cluster Network Model connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A workshop Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Trace Pod addressing, routing, CNI responsibilities, Service implementation, node paths, NAT, MTU, and platform-specific exceptions.

content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which K8s Cni Cluster Network Model condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: Kubernetes Services, Networking, DNS, and NetworkPolicy — Controls Pod networking, Services, EndpointSlices, DNS, traffic policies, dual stack, NetworkPolicy, ingress boundaries, source preservation, and layered diagnosis.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Network Layered Diagnosis
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-network-layered-diagnosis-rnvqt7". Tailor its network-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Network Layered Diagnosis.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Network Layered Diagnosis into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, keep K8s Network Layered Diagnosis connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-network-layered-diagnosis-rnvqt7
  - competency: k8s-network-layered-diagnosis
    artifact: network-policy.yaml
    decision: network layered diagnosis
    environment: shared multi-tenant cluster under Restricted Pod Security
    fault-injection: delete one ready Pod during a configuration rollout
    retained-proof: bound claim snapshot checksum and application consistency query
    case-key: rnvqt7
    change: |
      pod-network: cni-implementation-boundary
      ip-family-policy: PreferDualStack
      ingress: default-deny-plus-gateway
      egress: default-deny-plus-dns-and-api
      metadata-endpoint: denied
      return-path: stateful-implementation-verified
    verify: kubectl get networkpolicy,pod,service,endpointslice -n intake -o json
    failure: Reject policy override semantics, automatic return-path claims, DNS-success as endpoint proof, or a CNI-independent packet conclusion.
    repair: Derive additive ingress and egress allows, preserve DNS and required control paths, then test name, endpoint, route, policy, and application layers.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

content.value: Kubernetes Services, Networking, DNS, and NetworkPolicy — Controls Pod networking, Services, EndpointSlices, DNS, traffic policies, dual stack, NetworkPolicy, ingress boundaries, source preservation, and layered diagnosis.

hints: For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Network Layered Diagnosis precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Diagnose name, endpoint, bind, policy, route, proxy, gateway, certificate, and application failures with source and destination evidence.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Successful DNS resolution proves the Service has reachable ready backends.

stimulus.title: K8s Network Layered Diagnosis failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Successful DNS resolution proves the Service has reachable ready backends.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Diagnose name, endpoint, bind, policy, route, proxy, gateway, certificate, and application failures with source and destination evidence.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated K8s Network Layered Diagnosis condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Network Layered Diagnosis
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for K8s Network Layered Diagnosis: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop Pod can resolve DNS but cannot reach a backend after default deny; the policy selects only ingress, misses egress DNS, and assumes NetworkPolicy implementation behavior. Trace source, destination, port, direction, selector, CNI, and node boundary. Competency probe: Diagnose name, endpoint, bind, policy, route, proxy, gateway, certificate, and application failures with source and destination evidence.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Network Layered Diagnosis.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s Network Layered Diagnosis, keep K8s Network Layered Diagnosis connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Diagnose name, endpoint, bind, policy, route, proxy, gateway, certificate, and application failures with source and destination evidence.
